metadata.name: Test Engine Copilot Rules
metadata.description: Strict enforcement rules for React Training Project

projectOverview.description: Frontend learning-oriented training project using React 19, Vite, TypeScript, Node.js Express backend, and SQLite database.

rule.name: TypeScript Only
rule.requirement: All code MUST be written in TypeScript. NO EXCEPTIONS.
rule.enforcement: Reject any JavaScript files (.js). Accept only .ts and .tsx files.

rule.name: Feature Slice Pattern
rule.requirement: MANDATORY organization of all code using the feature slice pattern.
rule.enforcement: Every feature must follow the exact directory structure defined below.
rule.violation: CRITICAL - Feature must be restructured before merge

rule.name: No Direct API Calls
rule.requirement: Components MUST NEVER call API directly. ALWAYS use Redux + Saga.
rule.enforcement: Scan all component files for fetch, axios, or direct HTTP calls.
rule.violation: CRITICAL - Code rejection required, refactor using Saga middleware

fileOrganization.description: Each feature MUST follow this exact directory structure with no deviations.

enforcement.check: Verify every feature has all required files.
File names must match feature name exactly (case-sensitive).
No extra files allowed in feature directories.

fileOrganization.violation: HIGH - Feature structure must be corrected

actions.requirement: Define clear, specific action types in slices.
Action names MUST follow convention: FEATURE_NAME/ACTION_TYPE
actions.enforcement: Every action must be explicitly defined in the slice.
actions.violation: HIGH - Action must be properly defined

reducers.requirement: Keep reducers pure and simple. NO side effects.
Reducers must only handle state mutations using Immer.
reducers.enforcement: No async logic, no API calls, no external dependencies.
reducers.violation: HIGH - Reducer must be refactored to remove side effects

sagas.requirement: MANDATORY: Handle ALL side effects (API calls) in saga middleware.
Every API call must be wrapped in a saga.
Sagas must handle success, error, and loading states.
sagas.enforcement: No async/await in components or reducers. Saga-only.
sagas.violation: CRITICAL - Must refactor to use Saga

selectors.requirement: MANDATORY: Use selectors for accessing state.
Never access state directly in components.
Selectors must be memoized when selecting computed values.
selectors.enforcement: All state access through useSelector must use selector functions.
selectors.violation: MEDIUM - Refactor to use proper selector

rule.name: Functional Components Only
rule.requirement: ONLY functional components with hooks. NO class components.
rule.violation: CRITICAL - Must be converted to functional component

rule.name: Component Size
rule.requirement: Keep components small and focused. Max 300 lines per component.
rule.enforcement: Components exceeding 300 lines must be split.

rule.name: Form Validation
rule.requirement: Minimal validation for forms (this is a training project).
Validation must be consistent across all forms.

rule.name: TypeScript Interfaces
rule.requirement: MANDATORY: Use TypeScript interfaces for ALL props and state.
Never use 'any' type. Use proper typing.
rule.enforcement: Enable noImplicitAny and strict mode in tsconfig.
rule.violation: HIGH - Must add proper type definitions

rule.name: Saga Middleware Only
rule.requirement: ALL API calls MUST go through saga middleware.
rule.enforcement: No fetch, axios, or any HTTP client in components or reducers.
rule.violation: CRITICAL - Must refactor to use Saga

rule.name: Typed Responses
rule.requirement: API functions MUST return typed responses.
Define interfaces for all API response shapes.
rule.enforcement: Use strict TypeScript typing for all responses.
rule.violation: HIGH - Must add proper type definitions

rule.name: Error Handling
rule.requirement: Handle errors consistently across all features.
All API calls must have try-catch and dispatch error actions.
rule.enforcement: Every saga must handle success, error, and loading states.

rule.name: RESTful API Endpoints
rule.requirement: All endpoints MUST follow RESTful conventions.
rule.enforcement: Use correct HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE).
rule.violation: HIGH - Endpoint must follow REST standards

rule.name: Response Format
rule.requirement: MANDATORY: All responses must follow this format:
rule.enforcement: Every endpoint must return this exact structure.
rule.violation: CRITICAL - Response format must be corrected

rule.name: Express Middleware
rule.requirement: Use Express middleware for error handling and validation.
Centralized error handling middleware is MANDATORY.
rule.enforcement: All errors must pass through middleware before sending response.

rule.name: Database
rule.requirement: SQLite for data persistence.
All database queries must use parameterized statements.
rule.enforcement: No raw SQL concatenation. Use prepared statements.

rule.name: ESLint Compliance
rule.requirement: MANDATORY: Follow ESLint rules strictly with zero warnings.
rule.enforcement: No warnings allowed. All must be treated as errors.
rule.violation: MEDIUM - Must fix all ESLint issues

rule.name: Naming Conventions
rule.requirement: Use meaningful variable and function names.
NO single-letter variables except in loops.
Use camelCase for variables/functions, PascalCase for components/classes.

rule.name: Function Size
rule.requirement: Keep functions small and single-purpose.
Functions should do one thing only.
Max 50 lines per function (excluding tests).
rule.enforcement: Functions exceeding 50 lines must be split.

rule.name: Code Comments
rule.requirement: Add comments ONLY when necessary for complex logic.
Code should be self-documenting through clear naming.
rule.enforcement: Excessive comments indicate poor code clarity.
rule.violation: LOW - Remove unnecessary comments or improve code clarity

typeScriptStrictMode.enforcement: All TypeScript compiler options MUST be set as specified above.

level.name: CRITICAL
level.description: Code rejection required. Feature must be refactored before merge.

level.name: HIGH
level.description: Code review required. Must be fixed before merge.

level.name: MEDIUM
level.description: Should be fixed. Can be noted for improvement.

enforcement.description: These rules are NON-NEGOTIABLE in ULTRA_HIGH strict mode.
enforcement.requirement: ALL CRITICAL violations result in code rejection.
ALL HIGH violations must be resolved before merge.
Code reviews MUST validate every rule listed above.
No exceptions granted except by explicit project lead approval.
